lines.english: By Guru's Grace, bliss is obtained.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਸਿਮਰਤ ਮਨਿ ਹੋਇ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸਾ ਤਾ ਕੀ ਗਤਿ ਮਿਤਿ ਕਹਨੁ ਨ ਜਾਵੈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jis simarat man hoe pragaasaa taa kee gat mit kehan na jaavai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Meditating upon Him in remembrance, the mind is illumined; his state and condition cannot be described. ||1||Pause||
Meeting with them, love for God is embraced. ||1||
Fasts, religious vows, cleansing baths, and worship to Him;
listening to the Vedas, Puraanas, and Shaastras.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਜਾ ਕੈ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: saadhasangat jaa kai har har naam |2|
lines.english: who meditates upon the Name of the Lord, Har, Har, in the Saadh Sangat. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਹਾ ਪੁਨੀਤ ਜਾ ਕਾ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਥਾਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: mahaa puneet jaa kaa niramal thaan |
lines.english: Extremely pure is he, and immaculate is his place,
That humble being becomes renowned all over the world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਕੀ ਗਤਿ ਮਿਤਿ ਕਥਨੁ ਨ ਜਾਇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: taa kee gat mit kathan na jaae |3|
lines.english: his condition and state cannot be described. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਕਉ ਭੇਟਿਓ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਰਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: jaa kau bhettio har har raae |
lines.english: One who has met the Lord, the Lord our King,

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਤਿਤ ਪੁਨੀਤ ਤਾ ਕੀ ਪਗ ਰੇਨ ॥
lines.translit: patit puneet taa kee pag ren |
lines.english: Even sinners are purified, by the dust of his feet.
Twenty-four hours a day, with palms pressed together, I meditate;
I yearn to obtain the Blessed Vision of the Darshan of those Holy Saints.
Merge me, the poor one, with You, O Lord;
Twenty-four hours a day, he takes his cleansing bath in water;
Aasaa, Fifth Mehl:
Nanak has come to Your Sanctuary. ||4||38||89||
he makes continual offerings to the Lord; he is a true man of wisdom.
Again and again, he falls at the Lord's Feet. ||1||
He never leaves anything uselessly.
such is my worship, flower-offerings and divine adoration as well. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਘੰਟਾ ਜਾ ਕਾ ਸੁਨੀਐ ਚਹੁ ਕੁੰਟ ॥
lines.translit: ghanttaa jaa kaa suneeai chahu kuntt |
lines.english: His bell resounds to the four corners of the world.
Such is the Saalagraam, the stone idol, which I serve;

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਕਾ ਚਵਰੁ ਸਭ ਊਪਰਿ ਝੂਲੈ ॥
lines.translit: jaa kaa chavar sabh aoopar jhoolai |
lines.english: His chauri, his fly-brush, waves over all.
His seat is forever in heaven.
His incense is ever-fragrant. ||2||
The Saadh Sangat, the Company of the Holy, is His Eternal Court.
His Aartee, his lamp-lit worship service, is the Kirtan of His Praises, which brings lasting bliss.
His Greatness is so beautiful, and ever limitless. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਘਟਿ ਘਟਿ ਸੰਪਟੁ ਹੈ ਰੇ ਜਾ ਕਾ ॥
lines.translit: ghatt ghatt sanpatt hai re jaa kaa |
lines.english: He is treasured in each and every heart.
I hold in my hands the Saalagraam of the Lord.
he takes to the Sanctuary of the Saints' Feet.
He alone obtains it, who is so pre-ordained;
Aasaa, Fifth Mehl, Panch-Pada:
That highway, upon which the water-carrier is plundered
Says Nanak, the Guru has given me this Gift. ||4||39||90||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮ ਤੇਰੇ ਕੀ ਮੁਕਤੇ ਬੀਥੀ ਜਮ ਕਾ ਮਾਰਗੁ ਦੂਰਿ ਰਹਿਆ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: naam tere kee mukate beethee jam kaa maarag door rahiaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Your Name, O Lord, is the Way to Salvation; the road of the Messenger of Death is far away. ||1||Pause||
The True Guru has spoken the Truth.
- that way is far removed from the Saints. ||1||
That place, where the greedy toll-collector dwells

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੂਰਿ ਰਹੀ ਉਹ ਜਨ ਤੇ ਬਾਟ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: door rahee uh jan te baatt |2|
lines.english: - that path remains far removed from the Lord's humble servant. ||2||
Chitra and Gupat, the recording angels of the conscious and the unconscious, write the accounts of all mortal beings,
the Holy Saints remain with the Supreme Lord. ||3||
There, where so very many caravans of men are caught,
- the unblown bugles of ecstasy vibrate for him. ||5||40||91||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਗਤ ਜਨਾ ਕਉ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਨ ਪੇਖਾ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: bhagat janaa kau drisatt na pekhaa |4|
lines.english: but they cannot even see the Lord's humble devotees. ||4||
Says Nanak, one whose True Guru is Perfect
In the Saadh Sangat, the Company of the Holy, the Naam is learned;
Aasaa, Fifth Mehl, Du-Pada 1:
all desires and tasks are fulfilled.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬੁਝਿ ਗਈ ਤ੍ਰਿਸਨਾ ਹਰਿ ਜਸਹਿ ਅਘਾਨੇ ॥
lines.translit: bujh gee trisanaa har jaseh aghaane |
lines.english: My thirst has been quenched, and I am satiated with the Lord's Praise.
I have entered the Sanctuary of the Creator, the Cause of all causes.
I live by chanting and meditating upon the Lord, the Sustainer of the earth. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦਿ ਸਹਜ ਘਰੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਮਿਟਿਆ ਅੰਧੇਰਾ ਚੰਦੁ ਚੜਿਆ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: gur parasaad sehaj ghar paaeaa mittiaa andheraa chand charriaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: By Guru's Grace, I have entered the home of celestial bliss. Darkness is dispelled, and the moon of wisdom has risen. ||1||Pause||
